calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR SHORT CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-27-2020 Off Calendar

MOTION /Notice Of Motion And Motion To Discharge And Dismiss Stakeholder
Off Calendar Jan-28-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF FIDELITY NATIONAL TITLE COMPANY'S Motion To Discharge And Dismiss Stakeholder Pursuant to Code Civ. Procedure 386(b) 386.5.

Fidelity National Title Company's motion to discharge and dismiss is granted in part. The record demonstrates that there is no longer a dispute to the funds, Rece River Corp and Mark Ruiz have been defaulted in this action, and Jennifer Nushwat and Anton Missis are entitled to the $10,000. Therefore, there is nothing left to litigate in this action and the $10,000 (plus interest if any) shall be released to Jennifer Nushwat and Anton Missis. The court dismisses this action. The court exercises its discretion per Code of Civ. Proc., 386.5 and does not discharge Fidelity National Title Company's liability given the judgment in CGC-18-571523 and the paltry record that is presently before the court.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-21-2019 Set For Trial On Mar-30-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Dec-04-2019 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-30-2019 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ethan Friedman, Esq. 925-817-3704 of Fidelity National Law Group for Plaintiff through Court Call.
George Wynns, Esq. 415-279-7397 for Defendants JENNIFER NUSHWAT, NIMER MASSIS.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares its views. Defendant argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT JENNIFER NUSHWAT, NIMER MASSIS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT AFTER MEET AND CONFER; NOTICE OF INTENT TO MOVE FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST FIDELITY.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Jennifer Nushwat and Nimer Massis's demurrer is overruled. The demurrer improperly relies on extrinsic matter rather than on the pleadings and/or judicially noticeable matter alone. (CCP 430.30, 439.70.) Additionally, the facts as pled could "give rise to double or multiple liability." (CCP 386.) No sanctions are awarded to either side.

In addition, the Court grants the request for judicial notice.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
